userFacingSummaryDraft.oneSentenceSummary: Orlando Health offers 4-week clerkships to US MD/DO and international medical students; foreign students apply via paper with the Florida CIE 701/702 form, max 2 rotations at $50/rotation.
userFacingSummaryDraft.whoThisIsFor: Fourth-year medical students at US LCME-accredited institutions (via VSLO), AOA-accredited US DO institutions (via VSLO), LCME-accredited Canadian institutions (paper), or international medical schools (paper + CIE form). If your school holds CIE 702 Form, USMLE Step 1 AND Step 2 completion is required.
userFacingSummaryDraft.whoThisIsNotFor: IMG graduates (not explicitly stated); non-fourth-year students; foreign students whose school cannot obtain the CIE 701 or 702 form.
userFacingSummaryDraft.howToApply: US LCME/AOA students apply via VSLO. Non-VSLO students email the Orlando Health GME office for paper application. Foreign students additionally submit the CIE 701 or 702 form from their school via Florida Department of Education. Application package: CV, transcripts, $1M/$3M school malpractice proof, personal health insurance proof, $50/rotation fee. Min 4-6 weeks review.
userFacingSummaryDraft.estimatedCostSummary: $50 application/processing fee per approved rotation. School-provided malpractice $1M occurrence / $3M aggregate. No housing provided. Personal health insurance required.
keyCaveats: Foreign students require CIE 701 or 702 form (state of Florida regulatory)
CIE 702 Form holders need USMLE Step 1 + Step 2 completion before rotation start
4 weeks per rotation; 12 rotation dates published for 2026/2027 cycle
Specialty list: EM, General Surgery, IM, Neurology, OB/GYN, Ortho, Pathology, Pediatrics
Final approval contingent on state review of CIE form for foreign students
FSU, UF, USF are institutional affiliates
Visa specifics not discussed on source
userFacingSummaryDraft.whyWeClassifiedItThisWay: Source explicitly admits 'foreign medical students' on the same path as US students plus the CIE 701/702 form (Florida state regulatory requirement). Verbatim cost data ($50/rotation; $1M/$3M malpractice via home school) and explicit duration (4 weeks, max 2 rotations) anchor the classification.

negativeEvidence.whetherNegativeEvidenceStrong: STRONG positive evidence for international lane via CIE form.
negativeEvidence.futureRetrySuggestion: Search Florida Department of Education for current CIE 701/702 form template. Verify which AOA accreditation Orlando Health recognizes (AOA accreditation is now COCA under ACGME single-accreditation system).

changeDetectionPrep.notes: Rotation dates rotate annually; CIE form requirements are Florida state regulation and may change.

driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Orlando Health's explicit foreign-medical-student lane via Florida CIE 701/702 form (a state regulatory pathway) plus verbatim $50/rotation cost and 4-week-max-2 duration were documented with cleaned text and SHA-256 hash on the canonical T7 capsule; no DB/schema/noindex/homepage touched.
